about.title: Built for Modern Multilingual Marketing Websites
about.text: AppTemplate is a flexible Next.js 14 starter created for teams that need fast, SEO-friendly, and scalable landing pages with built-in internationalization from day one.
about.feature1: Next.js 14 foundation for modern performance and developer workflow
about.feature2: Built-in multilingual structure for global content delivery
about.feature3: SEO-ready architecture for marketing and landing pages
about.feature4: Clean component organization for fast customization
about.feature5: Flexible enough for startups, agencies, and in-house teams
about.feature6: Optimized for quick launch and long-term content scaling
about.extraParagraph: Launching a multilingual website usually requires balancing speed, structure, and maintainability. AppTemplate helps simplify that process with a ready-made foundation tailored to modern marketing use cases.
about.extraParagraph2: Instead of assembling routing, translations, page sections, and SEO settings from scratch, teams can start with a production-friendly base that already supports international growth.
about.extraParagraph3: The template is especially useful for product pages, SaaS websites, startup landing pages, and brand presentations that need to work across multiple regions and languages.
about.extraParagraph4: Its structure is designed to keep content easy to manage while preserving a polished frontend experience for visitors on desktop and mobile devices.
about.extraParagraph5: Because the project is based on Next.js 14, it fits naturally into modern frontend workflows and gives developers a clean starting point for further expansion.
about.extraParagraph6: Marketing teams benefit from a setup that is easy to edit, while developers benefit from reusable sections, scalable organization, and a reliable technical baseline.
about.extraParagraph7: Whether you are building a simple campaign page or a broader multilingual site, the template helps reduce setup time and keeps the project aligned with current best practices.
about.extraParagraph8: This makes AppTemplate a practical option for teams that want to move faster, publish globally, and maintain consistent quality across languages and markets.

extra2.title: Ready for Global Teams and Fast Product Launches
extra2.text: From localized landing pages to international product websites, AppTemplate gives teams a strong starting point with modern UI structure, scalable content organization, and deployment-ready architecture.

hero.title: Works With Your Modern Stack
hero.text: Connect your multilingual Next.js project with the tools, hosting platforms, and workflows your team already uses.

article.title: Built for Fast Integration and Simple Deployment
article.p1: This template is designed to fit naturally into modern frontend workflows, whether you deploy personal projects, marketing sites, or multilingual business pages.
article.p2: You can use it with common hosting providers, Git-based deployment pipelines, and standard localization workflows without restructuring your application from scratch.
article.highlight: Launch faster with a setup that works well with modern hosting, translation files, and scalable frontend tooling.
list: Ready for static hosting and CI/CD workflows
Easy integration with locale-based content structure
Flexible enough for personal, startup, and client projects
Clean project organization for long-term maintenance
article.p3: Whether you are starting a new multilingual landing page or adapting an existing website, the template keeps deployment and localization straightforward.

steps.title: Clone the Template
steps.description: Copy the project to your local environment and prepare it for your own domain, branding, and content.

steps.title: Install Dependencies
steps.description: Run your preferred package manager to install everything required for development, building, and localization.

steps.title: Configure Locales
steps.description: Add your languages, update translation resources, and organize content for each locale in a scalable structure.

steps.title: Deploy Anywhere
steps.description: Build the project and publish it to your preferred platform, from Vercel and Netlify to S3 and static hosting services.

banner.title: Flexible Deployment, Clean Workflow
banner.text: Use the same project structure across development, staging, and production with a setup that stays simple as your site grows.

articleSecurity.title: Performance and Best Practices
articleSecurity.p1: A well-structured multilingual project should not only be easy to deploy, but also fast to load, maintainable to update, and clear for teams working across multiple locales.
articleSecurity.p2: This setup supports practical frontend best practices that improve user experience while keeping the codebase organized for future development.
articleSecurity.highlight: Strong performance starts with clean structure, efficient assets, and predictable deployment workflows.
articleSecurity.p3: By combining static generation, optimized assets, and accessible markup, you can deliver a professional experience without adding unnecessary complexity.
list: Static generation for fast page delivery
Clear locale organization for easier maintenance
Optimized assets for better loading performance
Accessible structure for a better user experience

items.question: Can I deploy this project on different hosting platforms?
items.answer: Yes. The template is suitable for modern hosting services such as Vercel, Netlify, GitHub Pages, AWS S3, and other static-friendly platforms.

items.question: Is it suitable for multilingual websites?
items.answer: Yes. The structure is designed for locale-based content and translation files, making it practical for multilingual landing pages and websites.

items.question: Do I need to rebuild the project for every update?
items.answer: In most cases, yes. After content or translation changes, you typically rebuild and redeploy so the latest static output is published.

items.question: Can I use this for client and business projects?
items.answer: Absolutely. The setup is clean enough for portfolio sites, startup pages, product showcases, and multilingual business websites.

accordion.title: Start Your Project in a Few Clear Steps
accordion.subtitle: Follow a simple launch flow to configure your multilingual site, prepare content, and publish faster with a production-ready foundation.
accordion.intro: You can move from setup to launch quickly by following a small sequence of practical steps. Each step is designed to keep the project organized and easy to expand as your content grows.

items.title: Create Your Project Base
items.text: Start by cloning the repository and opening the project locally. This gives you the full starter structure, reusable sections, and the default multilingual setup in one place.

items.title: Install Dependencies
items.text: Run your preferred package manager to install all required dependencies. Once installed, you can launch the development environment and begin customizing the site immediately.

items.title: Configure Languages and Content
items.text: Add the locales your project needs, connect translation files, and update the page copy for each market. This step helps keep your multilingual structure scalable from the start.

items.title: Customize Sections and Branding
items.text: Replace the starter content with your own messaging, visuals, and brand styles. Because the layout is already structured for marketing pages, editing content stays fast and predictable.

items.title: Build and Deploy
items.text: When everything is ready, build the project and deploy it to your preferred hosting platform. The template is prepared for modern workflows, making launch and future updates easier to manage.
